summary: wallet-security-checker exfiltrates host/wallet data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: Package name advertises a wallet security tool but the shipped code is a credential and host-information stealer. scanner.js contains a hardcoded C2 at https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 161, 323) reached via https.request/https.get with POST and curl invocations, alongside execSync of host-recon commands (whoami, os.hostname()). build-verifier.js builds and POSTs structured payloads keyed by hostname/username/cwd (lines 316, 694, 1046, 1048) over https, and invokes child_process for system commands including 'docker push' (line 210). wallet-scanner.js performs further outbound POSTs (lines 4, 723, 795, 871) and shells out to curl/ping. The combination of (a) a hardcoded attacker-controlled C2 endpoint on a personal github.io subdomain unrelated to any legitimate publisher, (b) host/user/cwd enumeration assembled into the POST body, and (c) the package's stated purpose being wallet inspection (i.e., it is positioned to scan for wallet artifacts on the installer's machine) constitutes a credential/host-data exfiltration tool. Installing or running this package ships installer-side identifiers and any data the wallet scanner collects to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
